obligations.reason: The scope exposes external JTAG pins, dmi_jtag_tap DMI access signals, dmi_jtag authentication-related signals (pass_check, pass_mode, jtag_unlock_o, hashValid), and DMI request/response ports. Check whether the visible RTL prevents unauthenticated JTAG access from issuing or observing DMI transactions.
obligations.subject: external JTAG requester
obligations.operation: read/write DMI registers and receive DMI responses
obligations.object: debug module DMI interface and downstream debug capabilities
obligations.expected_guard: DMI request generation and response exposure should be gated by successful JTAG authentication/unlock and any required debug authorization, not merely by TAP DMI selection/update events.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG pins/tap -> dmi_jtag -> dmi_cdc -> debug module DMI request/response
state_conditions_to_check: conditions that set or clear authentication state
DMI access while authentication is pending or failed
DMI response shifting on TDO when locked
required_evidence_slots: operation: DMI read/write op encoding and valid/ready handshake
object: dmi_req/dmi_resp or debug module interface
observed behavior at request valid and response TDO mux
obligations.uncertainty: Need line-level RTL inspection to determine whether authentication signals actually gate dmi_req_valid_o, dmi_resp_ready_o, and TDO response shifting.

obligations.reason: The fact layer shows fuse_mem outputs both a 256-bit jtag_hash_o and 32-bit rdata_o with req_i/addr_i, plus riscv_peripherals fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata signals. Check whether the bus-visible fuse read path can expose the same protected hash/secret without permission mediation.
obligations.subject: software or network/MMIO requester
obligations.operation: read/expose fuse words or JTAG hash
obligations.object: fuse_mem contents and jtag_hash authentication reference value
obligations.expected_guard: Fuse/hash material used as a JTAG authentication secret should not be readable over ordinary software-visible or network/MMIO read paths unless guarded by an appropriate privilege/lifecycle/access-control check.
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default fuse contents or hash value
whether req_i is qualified by acc_ctrl/reglk/debug/priv signals
whether all fuse addresses or only non-secret words are readable
required_evidence_slots: object: fuse_mem rdata_o and jtag_hash_o storage/source
expected guard: privilege, lifecycle, lock, or access-control bit
path/reachability from top-level fabric to fuse_mem
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer identifies signals but not the full address decode or fuse_mem internals; inspect RTL to determine if rdata_o reveals protected hash material.

obligations.reason: riscv_peripherals has multiple NOC-facing peripheral channels, acc_ctrl, priv_lvl_i, debug_mode_i, and reglk_ctrl. Check that each protected peripheral decode path consistently applies the intended access-control bits and privilege/debug conditions before accepting transactions or returning protected data.
obligations.subject: software-visible NOC/MMIO requester
obligations.object: security-sensitive peripheral register banks and control interfaces in riscv_peripherals
obligations.expected_guard: Writes/reads to security-sensitive peripheral control/data registers should be mediated by acc_ctrl/priv_lvl/debug_mode/reglk_ctrl or equivalent access-control checks at the decode/use sites.
obligations.candidate_path: NOC/MMIO request ports -> riscv_peripherals address decode -> security-sensitive peripherals (AES/HMAC/SHA/RNG/RSA/DMA/debug/reset/fuse)
state_conditions_to_check: reads versus writes and byte-enable variants
required_evidence_slots: subject: NOC/MMIO requester and selected peripheral channel
operation: read/write enable and address decode
obligations.uncertainty: Need source inspection to map acc_ctrl bit indices to peripherals and identify which registers are protected.

obligations.reason: The design includes reglk_ctrl, a reglk NOC channel, and external we_flag_0..we_flag_4 inputs. Verify whether the lock state is truly protective rather than being writable/clearable or bypassed by alternate write enables.
obligations.subject: software/NOC requester and external write-flag sources
obligations.operation: set/clear lock bits and write locked registers
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl lock state and locked peripheral configuration registers
obligations.expected_guard: Register-lock bits should reset to a safe value, be set only by authorized writes, be sticky or only clearable by reset/authorized lifecycle event, and gate every write path to the protected registers they are intended to lock.
obligations.candidate_path: reglk NOC/MMIO channel and we_flag_* inputs -> reglk_ctrl update -> protected peripheral write gates
state_conditions_to_check: whether lock bits can transition 1->0 without reset
whether we_flag_* bypasses normal lock checks
whether lock is checked at final write-enable use site
required_evidence_slots: subject: software/NOC requester or external we_flag source
operation: lock update and protected register write
object: reglk_ctrl bits and specific locked registers
expected guard: lock bit/sticky behavior/authorized clear only
observed update equations and write gates
all alternate write paths including debug/test/external flags
obligations.uncertainty: Visible facts do not include update assignments; inspect sequential blocks around reglk_ctrl and protected register writes.

obligations.reason: dmi_jtag_tap has testmode_i and a DFT clock mux instance. Check whether testmode changes only clocking/test behavior or also creates an unguarded debug-access path.
obligations.subject: test/JTAG interface
obligations.operation: enable/route JTAG DMI shifting and output
obligations.object: TAP DMI-access control and TDO output path
obligations.expected_guard: Test mode/DFT clock selection should not enable DMI access, bypass authentication, or expose debug data on TDO except under intended manufacturing/lifecycle authorization.
obligations.candidate_path: dmi_jtag_tap testmode_i/DFT clock mux -> TAP state/control outputs -> DMI access and TDO
state_conditions_to_check: TDO output enable behavior in test mode
required_evidence_slots: operation: select DMI instruction, shift/capture/update, drive TDO
obligations.uncertainty: Presence of testmode is not sufficient; must inspect whether it affects permission-relevant muxes or state transitions.

obligations.reason: The TAP exposes dmi_reset_o and dmihardreset/dmireset fields, while dmi_jtag outputs dmi_rst_no and authentication-related state. Check reset lifecycle interactions so resets cannot be used as a permission bypass.
obligations.subject: external JTAG requester
obligations.object: DMI reset, debug module activity/reset outputs, and authentication state
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG-controlled DMI resets should not clear authentication/lock state into an unlocked condition or allow debug reset/control operations without the same authorization required for normal DMI access.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI reset controls dmi_reset/dmi_rst_no/dmihardreset -> authentication state and debug module reset/activity
state_conditions_to_check: whether reset can generate ndmreset/dmactive/debug_req side effects
required_evidence_slots: operation: DTMCS reset/hardreset or DMI reset control
object: authentication state, DMI interface, debug reset/activity outputs
expected guard: auth or safe locked reset state
observed reset effects and output behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Need sequential reset logic inspection to distinguish safe locked reset from bypass.

obligations.reason: dmi_jtag contains HMAC-related signals and an hmac instance with key_hash_bypass_i. Check whether unlock can be asserted through bypass, stale hashValid, partial pass_data loading, or invalid ready/digest sequencing.
obligations.subject: external JTAG requester
obligations.object: JTAG authentication state and HMAC comparison inputs/outputs
obligations.expected_guard: Authentication decision should depend on a valid completed HMAC/hash comparison against fuse-provided reference, with no unguarded bypass input or stale hash value permitting unlock.
state_conditions_to_check: behavior on failed attempts and subsequent retries
whether pass_data can be partially updated
required_evidence_slots: subject: JTAG requester controlling DMI scan data
operation: provide password/message and trigger authentication
expected guard: completed valid HMAC comparison to jtag_hash_i
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect dmi_jtag and hmac internal FSM to determine if bypass is constant or externally controllable.

obligations.reason: The top module exposes ariane_dma NOC ports alongside access-control signals. Check whether DMA-originated accesses are mediated and cannot target protected peripherals or debug/fuse/security regions without authorization.
obligations.subject: DMA engine and software configuring DMA
obligations.object: DMA master path and protected memory/peripheral address regions
obligations.expected_guard: DMA configuration and active transfers should be constrained by access-control/range checks equivalent to CPU privilege/PMP or peripheral access policy, and config updates should not race active transfers to escape bounds.
obligations.candidate_path: DMA NOC/MMIO configuration -> DMA master transactions -> protected address ranges/peripherals
state_conditions_to_check: DMA config writes under different privilege/access bits
active transfer while config registers change
required_evidence_slots: observed address decode and master request behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows DMA ports but not internal DMA implementation; inspect visible top-level routing/access checks.

obligations.reason: Top-level debug outputs are security-sensitive control effects. Check all visible requester paths (debug NOC and JTAG DMI) for proper authorization before they can assert debug control signals.
obligations.subject: JTAG requester or software/NOC debug requester
obligations.object: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o and debug module control state
obligations.expected_guard: Requests that halt harts, activate debug mode, or assert non-debug-module reset should require debug authorization and should not be triggerable by ordinary unprivileged MMIO/NOC traffic or unauthenticated JTAG.
obligations.candidate_path: debug NOC/MMIO channel and external JTAG DMI -> debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o outputs
state_conditions_to_check: reset defaults and persistence of dmactive
required_evidence_slots: subject: NOC debug requester or JTAG requester
observed control equations and request decode
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect debug module instantiation/wiring in riscv_peripherals for precise control path.

obligations.reason: riscv_peripherals includes a reset NOC channel and many reset signals. Check whether reset writes are permission-guarded and whether they can clear lock/access-control/security state improperly.
obligations.subject: software/NOC reset requester
obligations.object: rst_1..rst_13 controlled peripheral resets and affected security state
obligations.expected_guard: Software-triggered resets of security-sensitive peripherals should be authorized and should not reset lock/access-control/authentication state into a permissive condition or leave residue externally observable.
obligations.candidate_path: reset peripheral NOC/MMIO channel -> rst_1..rst_13 signals -> peripheral resets/security state
state_conditions_to_check: which rst_* resets feed security-sensitive modules
ability to reset one peripheral while retaining stale outputs/data
required_evidence_slots: object: reset lines and affected protected registers/peripherals
observed reset generation and affected state defaults
obligations.uncertainty: Need identify mapping from rst_* to peripherals and reset register decode.
